free_text: Preliminary mass yields given in figs
10 MeV electron linac of the Electron
Pu-240: 90 ug, Pu nitrate solution, area 1.0cm2
All the samples were wrapped in 0.025 mm super pure
The detector resolution was 2.0 keV for 1332 keV gamma
Irradiated for 4-5 hrs, cooled for 0.5-1.5 hrs
Fission products were measured by using a
recoil catcher and off-line gamma-ray spectrometric
Most probable charge, charge dispersion and even-odd
effect factor (EOF) are assumed to derive chain yield
The electron beam pulse parameters were 50 mA, 400 Hz,
contributions from both random and systematic errors.
The random error in the observed activity is due to
counting statistics and is estimated to be 10-15%.
- Half-life of nuclides of fission products (~1%)
H.Naik and K.C. Jagadeesan, BARC, Mumbai
